arguments.task: Open the Reducto website and find official first-party pages that document the API, authentication methods, pricing/access, and any MCP/server documentation. Prioritize developer docs, API reference, auth docs, and pricing pages. Open the strongest canonical pages and extract the relevant text snippets and final URLs. If docs are JS-rendered, wait for them to load. Return the visited URLs and concise extracted evidence for description, auth methods, access tier, API surface, rate limits, and MCP availability.

input.thought: Use the browser to inspect official Reducto pages and capture evidence from live first-party docs.

raw_result.description: AI document-processing platform for parsing, extracting, splitting, classifying, and editing enterprise documents via API, SDKs, CLI, and MCP.
raw_result.auth_notes: The Quickstart documents API keys generated in Reducto Studio and used as REDUCTO_API_KEY for API/SDK access. The MCP server docs also document a one-time login flow via `uvx mcp-server-reducto --login`, which stores credentials locally in ~/.reducto/config.yaml.
raw_result.gating_reason: Reducto publishes self-serve pricing with a Standard pay-as-you-go tier that includes the APIs and provides the first 15k credits free before usage charges apply.

api_surface.breadth: Document-processing REST API with endpoints/capabilities for parse, extract, split, classify, edit, job polling, plus official Python, Node.js, and Go SDKs, a CLI, and a hosted/local MCP server.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Reducto MCP Server docs describe local and hosted MCP usage for clients such as Claude and Cursor, with tools including upload_file, parse_document, extract_data, split_document, classify_document, and edit_document.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs list edge rate limits of 1,000 requests/second sustained for endpoints and 200 requests/second for polling /job status; exceeding those returns HTTP 429. Separate concurrency throttling limits parallel parse batches by tier (for example Standard 200 in US, Growth 350, Enterprise 500+), causing queueing rather than 429s.
raw_result.notable_signals: First-party docs are substantial and current-looking, with dedicated overview, quickstart, API reference, rate-limit, throttling, and MCP pages, plus explicit SDKs and hosted MCP endpoint documentation.

result.description: AI document-processing platform for parsing, extracting, splitting, classifying, and editing enterprise documents via API, SDKs, CLI, and MCP.
result.auth_notes: The Quickstart documents API keys generated in Reducto Studio and used as REDUCTO_API_KEY for API/SDK access. The MCP server docs also document a one-time login flow via `uvx mcp-server-reducto --login`, which stores credentials locally in ~/.reducto/config.yaml.
result.gating_reason: Reducto publishes self-serve pricing with a Standard pay-as-you-go tier that includes the APIs and provides the first 15k credits free before usage charges apply.

api_surface.breadth: Document-processing REST API with endpoints/capabilities for parse, extract, split, classify, edit, job polling, plus official Python, Node.js, and Go SDKs, a CLI, and a hosted/local MCP server.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Reducto MCP Server docs describe local and hosted MCP usage for clients such as Claude and Cursor, with tools including upload_file, parse_document, extract_data, split_document, classify_document, and edit_document.

result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs list edge rate limits of 1,000 requests/second sustained for endpoints and 200 requests/second for polling /job status; exceeding those returns HTTP 429. Separate concurrency throttling limits parallel parse batches by tier (for example Standard 200 in US, Growth 350, Enterprise 500+), causing queueing rather than 429s.
result.notable_signals: First-party docs are substantial and current-looking, with dedicated overview, quickstart, API reference, rate-limit, throttling, and MCP pages, plus explicit SDKs and hosted MCP endpoint documentation.
